assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 shows: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate, 11/13); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A (no test files). Test Documentation: N/A (no test files). |'. The Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by the Eval Quality sub-component being WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-29 show explicit eval result detection: 'Detected 1 eval result review: - **Review ID 40001** from `github-actions[bot]` -- matched all 3 detection criteria: 1. Author is `github-actions[bot]` 2. Body contains `## Eval Results` 3. Body contains `sdlc-workflow/run-evals`'. The Eval Metrics table (lines 35-43) and Failing Assertions section (lines 45-51) confirm eval metrics were extracted and processed.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 from reviewer-b as a 'suggestion' with reasoning about reviewer language ('Consider adding'). The classification does not reference eval detection, eval metrics, pass rates, or any eval-related processing. It is treated as a standard human review comment. The report.md line 5 confirms: '1 human review comment classified as suggestion (comment 50001)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes: 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate, 11/13); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56)'. Lines 45-51 detail the two failing assertions: (1) 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002' with evidence about missing CONVENTIONS.md lookup, and (2) 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path' with evidence about no sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists and targets eval-3 assertion failures. Its Description states: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation for review comment 30002. The eval results show 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (11/13 passed).' The Review Context section (lines 30-38) includes both failing assertion texts and their evidence.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 29-38 contain a '## Review Context' section with: **Assertion 1:** 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with **Evidence:** 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. **Assertion 2:** 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with **Evidence:** 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 39 contains: '## Target PR' followed by line 39: 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. This matches the required URL exactly.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6 shows: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval-3 failures traced to implement-task phase: convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for suggestion-classified comments |'. The verdict is DONE (not N/A). Lines 57-67 provide the full Root-Cause Investigation section with defect description, universality test, method-vs-fact test, classification ('Skill gap -- implement-task phase'), and phase analysis.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis and report files: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, and criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. These are all verification outputs (classifications, evaluations, sub-task descriptions, and the final report). No code files (e.g., .ts, .py, .rs, .md skill files) were modified. The report itself confirms the PR modifies only 2 files (style-conventions.md and SKILL.md) which are the files under review, not files modified by the skill.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows independent non-eval checks: Scope Containment (line 7): 'PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' -- compares diff files against task spec. Sensitive Patterns (line 10): 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines' -- scans diff for secrets. Acceptance Criteria (line 12): 'PASS | 7 of 7 criteria met' with detailed breakdown in lines 69-79 showing each criterion evaluated against diff content (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md confirm independent evaluation of each criterion against the PR diff, with no reference to eval results in their reasoning).
